Your Healing Kitchen
Natural healing · Fourth trimester
Tonic • Blood-building • Postpartum recovery

Jujube & Goji Tea

A sweet, ruby tea of red dates and goji — gently rebuilds the blood after birth.
Makes 3 cups
Time 20 min
Caffeine none
For you · a warming daily brew
When to drink Daytime — a warm cup mid-morning or afternoon. Naturally sweet, caffeine-free, gentle any day.

Why this heals you

  • Red dates (jujube) — traditionally nourish blood & energy after birth tradition
  • Goji berries — rich in iron & antioxidants tradition
  • Naturally sweet — no added sugar needed tradition
  • Warming & caffeine-free — easy on a depleted system tradition
Ingredients
6, splitRed dates (jujube)
1 tbspGoji berries
3 cupsFiltered water
2 slicesFresh ginger (optional)
Method
1
Split the dates

Tear or slit the 6 red dates so they open — this releases their sweetness.

2
Simmer

Add the dates and ginger to 3 cups water. Simmer gently for 15 min.

3
Add goji & steep

Stir in 1 tbsp goji, simmer 5 more min. Sip warm — eat the softened fruit too.

Just for you, Mum

The classic postpartum ‘confinement’ brew — sweet, warming and iron-rich to help rebuild what birth took. Make a batch and sip through the day.

Good to know
BestWarm through the day; batch and gently reheat.
SweeterAdd 2 more dates — no sugar needed.
Why we say this
Red-date & goji brews are a long-standing East-Asian postpartum tradition for nourishing blood; honestly labelled tradition, not a clinical claim.
Main Meals
Snacks
Drinks
Desserts
Food, not medical advice · non-diagnostic · made for this season of motherhood
